titleStmt.title: Postcard from Mary Macken to Hanna Sheehy Skeffington, 16 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Mary Macken

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Postcard from Mary Margaret Macken to Hanna Sheehy Skeffington (1877-1946). Mary Margaret Macken apologizes for missing a lecture, writing that she received the invitation sent by Hanna Sheehy Skeffington after she had made other plans.Hanna Sheehy Skeffington, suffragette, nationalist, language teacher, was the widow of Francis Sheehy-Skeffington (who was summarily executed on 26 April 1916). She was active during the Rising, bringing food to the Volunteers in the G.P.O. and the College of Surgeons. Four days passed before she found out what had happened to her husband, Francis (1878-1916), and it wasn't until almost two weeks later that the full details of his execution emerged.

msIdentifier.collection: Sheehy Skeffington Papers, Collection List No. 47;, MS 33,604/16-10

correspAction.placeName: Westmoreland Chambers, 34 Westmoreland St., Co. Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
61, Dartmouth Square 16.2.16 I am sorry that I
was not able to go to the
lecture last evening. Your
postcard did not arrive
until Monday morning, & I
had then made a previous
arrangement for last evening y=Yours sincerely M.M. Macken.
Mrs. Sheehy Skeffington Irish Women's Franchise League Westmoreland Chambers 34, Westmoreland St. Dublin
